qpos.notes: 6 arm joints + 1 normalized gripper each arm (left[7] + right[7])

env_state.notes: cube/peg pose [xyz + quat wxyz]; sim_insertion has 14 (two objects)

action_spec.controller: ALOHA bimanual ViperX position control (14-dim: 7 per arm × 2)
action_spec.notes: MuJoCo actuator count is 16 (each finger has mirrored pos/neg constrained actuators), but the actual control surface — the API users + ALOHA-trained policies see — is 14-dim (6 joints + 1 gripper per arm × 2 arms). `sim_env.step(action)` accepts (14,) and internally mirrors finger to actuator pairs.

extra_notes: ACT (Action Chunking Transformer) bimanual ALOHA. Env action_spec = 16-D mujoco actuator inputs (6 joints + 2 finger widths per arm). Proprio qpos = 14-D (6 joints + 1 normalized gripper each arm). The policy_websocket server in tests/test_random_policy_server.py advertises action_dim=14 (matches qpos shape); scripts/run_demo.py and scripts/run_eval.py pad 14→16 before env.step. Reward is dm_env sparse stage indicator (0..env_max_reward) — see env._task; sim_transfer_cube max=4, sim_insertion max=4. [gripper indices] bimanual ViperX: index=6 is left finger pos (primary); paired antagonistic pair at idx=7. Right arm: idx=14 primary, idx=15 antagonist. Spec is hand-curated (auto-introspection of dm_control envs returns the 16-D raw actuator surface, not the 14-D policy interface). Cross-task verified manually against sim_insertion_scripted: same action_spec and obs_spec layout (env_state shape changes from 7 to 14 for two objects, treated as task-specific by spec contract).
